source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/04_제품별 인허가 진행 문서/01_HAD1717MC(MG)/▶ CE MDD/■ 3. 3차 CE TCF 보안사항 제출 (12월10일)/06. Clinical evaluation report/A-CLSR-01 Clinical Literature Searching report 20.03.03.pdf

sourceSummary.oneLine: Clinical literature search report for Digital Flat Panel X-ray detector models HAD1717MG and HAD1717MC.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports clinical evaluation by documenting literature search strategy, selection criteria, appraisal criteria, selected literature, and performance and safety evidence for the device.
keyPoints: The report covers Digital flat panel x-ray detector models HAD1717MC and HAD1717MG.
The stated intended use is a digital imaging solution for general radiographic systems for human anatomy, replacing film or screen based radiographic systems in general purpose diagnostic procedures.
The devices are not to be used for mammography.
Literature search sources included ScienceDirect, PubMed/NCBI, and Google.
The literature search date range was 2019.07.16 to 2020.03.03, with period covered by search from 2000 to 2020.03.03.
The report uses suitability and data contribution appraisal criteria for performance and safety literature.

concepts.name: Clinical equivalence
concepts.evidence: Clinical equivalence - Similar Condition of use (IFU) - Same clinical condition - Same intended purpose - Application site on the body - Target population - Same side effect

concepts.name: Biological equivalence
concepts.evidence: Biological equivalence - Same material - Contact with same body fluid or tissue - Same duration of contact

evidence.claim: The document is a Clinical Literature Search Report.
The report number is A-CLSR-01 Rev.2.
The device name and models are Digital flat panel x-ray detector/HAD1717MC,MG.
evidence.evidenceText: 1.1 device name/model: Digital flat panel x-ray detector/HAD1717MC,MG

evidence.claim: HAD1717MC and HAD1717MG are indicated for digital imaging solution designed for general radiographic system for human anatomy.
evidence.evidenceText: HAD1717MC and HAD1717MG are indicated for digital imaging solution designed for general radiographic system for human anatomy.

evidence.claim: The devices are intended to replace film or screen based radiographic systems in general purpose diagnostic procedures.
evidence.evidenceText: It is intended to replace film or screen based radiographic systems in all general purpose diagnostic procedures.

evidence.claim: The devices are not to be used for mammography.
evidence.evidenceText: Not to be used for mammography.

evidence.claim: The literature search was performed from 2019.07.16 to 2020.03.03.
The search period covered literature from 2000 to 2020.03.03.
Kim Wan Su undertook the literature search.
evidence.evidenceText: Name of person undertaking the literature search Kim Wan Su

evidence.claim: Literature sources included ScienceDirect, PubMed/NCBI, and Google.
evidence.evidenceText: Literature source used to identify data A. http://www.sciencedirect.com B. http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed C. http://www.google.com

evidence.claim: Search keywords included Digital flat panel x-ray detector, Flat panel detector, Digital radiography, Image quality, Digital x-ray, and Digital Radiographic Diagnosis.
evidence.evidenceText: Search terms (Key words) A. Digital flat panel x-ray detector B. Flat panel detector C. Digital radiography D. Image quality E. Digital x-ray F. Digital Radiographic Diagnosis

evidence.claim: The criteria for evaluating clinical data value are stated as based on MEDDEV 2.7/1 Rev4-A6.
evidence.evidenceText: The criteria for evaluating the value of clinical data are based on the case of a trial lacking scientific validity to demonstrate adequate clinical performance and clinical safety in accordance with MEDDEV 2.7/1 Rev4-A6.

evidence.claim: Clinical investigations are expected to be designed, conducted, and reported in accordance with EN ISO 14155 or a comparable standard, local regulations, and the Declaration of Helsinki.
evidence.evidenceText: Clinical investigations are generally expected to be designed, conducted and reported in accordance with EN ISO 14155 or to a comparable standard, and in compliance with local regulations and the Declaration of Helsinki.

evidence.claim: The report states suitable literature was found and literatures from scientific databases met the acceptable criteria.
evidence.evidenceText: We suitable literature was found. As the results of the literature search, the literatures from scientific databases are met the acceptable criteria.

reviewFlags.reason: Authority or jurisdiction is not explicitly identified in the document text; source metadata authoritySignal says FDA, while the source path references CE MDD and the text references MEDDEV.
Revision history lists Rev. 1 date as Feb/03/2019 after Rev. 0 date Sep/05/2019, which appears chronologically inconsistent.
Some wording appears OCR or source quality affected, including 'We suitable literature was found' and repeated/awkward phrases.
No explicit issuing regulatory authority, notified body, approval status, or certificate number is present in the source text.
